evals.prompt: Build me a 5-slide intro deck on reinforcement learning.
evals.expected_output: A .pptx file built from a chosen template with title slide, 3 content slides, and a closing slide. Output includes the template name, slide-by-slide layout list, and a download URL.
assertions: container_initialize is called before any template / exec call
container_template_list (or _categories) is called to choose a template
container_template_info is called for the chosen template before slides are written
python-pptx is pip-installed in the container
The build script is written via container_file_write, not via inline exec
container_file_download_url is called and the URL is returned to the user
The user is told that the download URL has a TTL

evals.prompt: Use the 'minimal' template to render this outline: Title / 3 bullets / closing.
evals.expected_output: Skips template discovery, fetches container_template_info('minimal') directly, renders 3 slides matching the outline, returns download URL.
assertions: container_template_info is called with template_name='minimal' (or the named template)
container_template_list is NOT called (template is already named)
Layouts match the outline (title, title+content, title)

evals.prompt: Build a deck and include this image as the second slide: <image_url>.
evals.expected_output: Image is uploaded to the container first, deck is built referencing the local image path, picture-with-caption layout is used.
assertions: Image is fetched and written to the container via container_file_write or container_file_upload_url
python-pptx reads the image from the local container path, not from a URL
The slide uses a picture-with-caption layout (or similar)
